note: Truncated/prefix binary scan: scan only the first N dims of the binary code (--scan-bits), rerank with full f32. Less scan bandwidth -> higher QPS; recall recovered by rerank up to a point. Scan is bandwidth-bound: 512/1024 bits ~doubles scan QPS. Cohere v3 is NOT Matryoshka-trained so prefix dims aren't the most informative; near-iso-recall sweet spot is 768/C=2000 (+23% QPS, recall 0.987 vs full 0.994). All rows same run/conditions; compare within table.
